items.headline: Dembélé bags a first-half hat-trick as France romp past Norway
items.body: So much for the Mbappé-vs-Haaland billing — Ousmane Dembélé stole the whole show. The PSG star ripped off all three of his goals inside the first 32 minutes in Boston, the first first-half hat-trick at a men's World Cup since 1994, as France beat a rotated Norway 4-1 to win Group I. Next up: Sweden in the Round of 32.
items.say: Everyone tuned in for Mbappé vs. Haaland and Dembélé went and scored a hat-trick in the first half hour instead. France looked scary.

items.headline: Group stage is done — the World Cup knockouts begin Sunday
items.body: The bracket is set and the single-elimination madness starts tomorrow, kicking off with South Africa vs. Canada in L.A. The big ones land fast: Brazil-Japan and Germany-Paraguay on Monday, USMNT vs. Bosnia on Wednesday, then France-Sweden and an eventual Argentina-Cape Verde tie. Win or go home from here.
items.say: Group stage's over — knockouts start tomorrow. The U.S. plays Bosnia Wednesday. You filling out a bracket?

links.text: Sky Sports: the full knockout bracket

items.headline: Russell snatches a wild Austrian pole as Verstappen crashes at home
items.body: Drama at the Red Bull Ring: George Russell pipped the two Ferraris of Leclerc and Hamilton to pole on the very last lap — moments after Max Verstappen buried his car in the gravel at Turn 9 in front of his home fans. Russell's lap came through yellow flags and was briefly investigated before stewards let it stand. Race is Sunday.
items.say: Verstappen crashed out of qualifying at his home race and Russell nicked pole on the final lap. Austria's always chaos — the race is Sunday.

links.text: Formula 1: Verstappen crashes heavily in Q3
Formula1.com: Russell beats the Ferraris to pole

items.headline: Giannis is a Miami Heat — Bucks finally pull the trigger
items.body: The summer's biggest domino fell: Milwaukee traded two-time MVP Giannis Antetokounmpo to Miami in a blockbuster that beat out Boston. The Bucks land a haul headlined by Tyler Herro, Kel'el Ware and Jaime Jaquez Jr. plus a stack of first-round picks. The Heat suddenly have a title-level centerpiece.
items.say: Did you see Giannis got traded to the Heat? Miami beat out Boston for him. That whole saga's finally over — Milwaukee got a ton of picks back.

items.headline: Paul Skenes whiffs his 500th career batter — fastest Pirate ever
items.body: The flamethrower keeps making history: Pittsburgh's Paul Skenes notched the 500th strikeout of his career against the Reds, getting there in just 72 starts — the quickest any Pirates pitcher ever has, passing franchise legend Bob Veale. He's must-watch every fifth day even when the wins won't come.
items.say: Paul Skenes just hit 500 career strikeouts faster than any Pirate in history. The guy throws 100 and it's appointment viewing every start.

links.text: MLB: Skenes fans seven in five innings

items.headline: 'Squid Game' drops its final season today
items.body: Game over — for real this time. The third and final season of Netflix's global juggernaut lands today, picking up with Gi-hun's last, bloody stand against the Front Man. Expect new games, higher stakes, and yes, that giant Red Light, Green Light doll is back. The whole season's up to binge.
items.say: The last season of Squid Game just dropped — I know what I'm doing this weekend. You caught up, or starting over?

items.headline: 'Supergirl' lands a softer-than-hoped opening; 'Toy Story 5' still rules
items.body: Milly Alcock's cape debut pulled in a so-so opening weekend — somewhere around $40M, below what DC was hoping for — as 'Toy Story 5' shrugged it off and held onto the No. 1 spot in its second week. Still a fun watch if you're in a superhero mood; just not the knockout some expected.
items.say: Supergirl opened kind of quietly — Toy Story 5 stayed No. 1 over it. Did you end up seeing either this weekend?

links.text: Deadline: Supergirl & Toy Story 5 box office

items.headline: Boards of Canada are back with 'Inferno' — their first album in 13 years
items.body: One of electronic music's most mysterious acts resurfaced after over a decade away. The Scottish duo's fifth album 'Inferno' (on Warp) arrived following a wonderfully cryptic rollout — unmarked VHS tapes mailed to old fans, posters in random cities — and that warm, hazy, slightly eerie sound is right back. Headphones recommended.
items.say: Boards of Canada finally put out a new album — first one in 13 years. They even mailed fans mystery VHS tapes to tease it. Very them.

links.text: NPR: BoC's first album in 13 years

items.headline: Justin Bieber surprise-drops a live album from his Coachella comeback
items.body: Out of nowhere, Bieber released 'SWAG Live From Coachella (Weekend I)' — a 22-track recording of the record-breaking set that became the most-watched performance in festival history. Guests Tems, Wizkid, The Kid LAROI and Mk.gee all show up. Yes, it's the same show he got a little roasted for playing YouTube clips during.
items.say: Bieber surprise-dropped a whole live album from that massive Coachella set — the one everyone was talking about. 22 tracks, a bunch of guests.
